programs.title: The Glassworker
programs.desc: Vincent Oliver, the son of renowned glassmaker Tomasz, is preparing for his first exhibition when he finds a letter from his childhood friend, Alliz Amano, whom he has always loved.He recalls how - raised by a pacifist father - he saw her for the first time.Alliz arrives in Vincent's city, Waterfront, with her parent, Colonel Amano.They become a couple.Meanwhile, a war breaks out and both he and his rival for the girl's favor, Malik, join the army.Tomasz is forced to produce glass rectifiers for the army.Some time later, the girl's father dies without any news at the front.When Malik returns to town with him, Alliz rewards him with a kiss, which Vincent witnesses.Devastated, he sabotages the production of rectifiers, but this leads to tragedy.The anime-inspired production with an anti-war message is the first Pakistani film made using the hand-animated technique.

programs.title: Twilight
programs.desc: Seventeen-year-old Bella Swan moves in with her father, who is the sheriff of a small town.At her new school, the teenager notices Edward Cullen.The unnaturally pale boy, who has the ability to read people's minds, is an inaccessible loner.Hoping that this will scare Bella away, Edward confesses to her that he is a vampire and that he and his entire family have been feeding on animal blood for centuries.This shocking truth does not discourage a girl in love.She and Cullen become inseparable.Their love, however, is threatened by a primal predatory instinct that is increasingly difficult for the boy to control.The couple is surrounded by wanderers hungry for human blood: Laurent, James and Victoria.The lovers face them.

programs.title: Your Monster
programs.desc: Actress Laura (Melissa Barrera) was supposed to become a star of the stage and live happily with the director of the show, Jacob (Edmund Donovan), but her beloved dumped her and took the role away.The devastated woman returns to her family home, where she spends all day in a bathrobe and crying.Her wailing gets on the nerves of the monster (Tommy Dewey), who has been living in the closet for years.The monster gives Laura two weeks to move out and threatens bloody revenge.Over time, however, an extraordinary bond develops between them, which helps Laura open her heart and follow her dreams.

programs.title: 30 days of darkness
programs.desc: The town of Barrow, Alaska, goes dark for thirty days every year.Most residents leave their homes at this time.The safety of those who decided to stay is ensured by young sheriff Eben Olsen (Josh Hartnett).He is struggling with serious personal problems.His marriage with Stella (Melissa George) is in crisis and she wants to leave him.Soon, however, they must unite their forces and fight the vampires who attack a town cut off from the world and plunged in darkness.Hungry monsters launch a bloody hunt for people imprisoned in Barrow.Eben, Stella and a handful of survivors begin their fight for survival.They do everything to wait for the end of the polar night.Adapted from a comic book by Steve Niles and Ben Templesmith.

programs.title: Last Swim
programs.desc: Ziba (Deba Hekmat), whose family comes from Iran, lives in London and is an ambitious young woman.However, she hides a painful secret that none of her friends know about.Ziba has just gotten into university, where she is going to study astrophysics.On this occasion, he meets with his friends: Tara (Lydia Fleming), Shea (Solly McLeod) and Merf (Jay Lycurgo), with whom he intends to celebrate this event.She feels irritated when she learns that they are to be joined by a promising young football player, Malcolm (Denzel Baidoo), whom she does not know.Soon, however, an event occurs that makes him emotionally closer to her than her long-time friends.The future for both of them is not as bright as it might seem.Sasha Nathwani's feature-length directorial debut.

programs.title: Killer Heat
programs.desc: Greece.American Nick Bali (Joseph Gordon-Levitt) is hired to investigate the circumstances of the death of young shipping magnate Leo Vardakis (Richard Madden) in Crete.The deceased's sister-in-law, Penelope Vardakis (Shailene Woodley), does not believe the official police report.A private detective discovers that sunny landscapes hide many dark secrets and anyone can be a suspect.He discovers further facts about the rich and influential Vardakis family, as well as the brothers' complicated relationship.Adaptation of the short story "The Jealousy Man" by Jo Nesbo.
